posts.title: Under the Hood: Introducing the VulnRap Engine Deep-Dive Series
posts.summary: Update #11 — A guided tour through each scoring engine: what it measures, how it votes, and where the boundaries are.

posts.title: Bring Your Own Fixtures: Validating VulnRap Against Your Corpus
posts.summary: Update #10 — Run VulnRap's scoring pipeline against your own labelled reports and compare precision/recall on your data.

posts.title: Showing Our Work: Per-Signal Precision/Recall in the UI
posts.summary: Update #9 — Every signal now surfaces its own precision and recall numbers so you can see exactly which detectors pull their weight.

posts.title: VulnRap Now Speaks MCP: Triage From Inside Your Agent
posts.summary: Update #13 — VulnRap exposes its scoring, similarity, and diagnostics tooling over the Model Context Protocol so agents like Claude Desktop can triage reports without leaving the chat.

posts.title: Three Ways Slop Hid This Sprint (And How the Audit Caught Them)
posts.summary: Sprint 14 — Three classes of slop slipped past the scorer this sprint. Here is how the disagreement-floor audit, response-body strip-and-retest, and ARM64 register-dump detector pulled them back into the red zone.

posts.title: Closing the Slop Gap: Engine 3 Now Cross-Checks Engine 2 Before It Votes
posts.summary: Sprint 12 — The substance LLM now refuses to outvote the heuristic on evidence-free reports. A six-condition AND-gate, an evidence-free cap, and a calibration audit that drops llmHigherCount on slop by 83%.

posts.title: AVRI: One Rubric Wasn't Enough — Why v3.7.0 Scores Memory Bugs and XSS Slop on Different Yardsticks
posts.summary: Sprint 11 — The Adversarial Vulnerability Report Index splits scoring by family so a memory-corruption report and an XSS report no longer share the same gold-signal yardstick.

posts.title: v3.5.0 Field Test: Three Engines, Seven Reports, and a Ceiling We Didn't Expect
posts.summary: Field test of v3.5.0 across seven real-world reports surfaced a scoring ceiling we did not predict. What the three engines agreed on, where they disagreed, and what the next sprint changes.

posts.title: 460K Vulnerability Reports, 56 Sources, and a 0% Detection Rate
posts.summary: The week we built the most diverse vulnerability report dataset we could find, ran our scoring system against it, and watched it score AI slop and real CVEs identically. Plus: what the data taught us about evidence over style, and the eleven-engine architecture replacing the formula.

posts.title: The Data Is There, the Score Isn't Listening
posts.summary: Sprint 8 results: the pipeline finally works, substance detection is live, and the scoring formula mathematically cannot use it. Plus: prompt injection defense, the LLM blind spot, and why we keep getting our own system wrong.

posts.title: Grading AI with AI: What We Learned When Our Detection Model Met the Real World
posts.summary: Six development sprints, a 20% real-world detection rate, and the fundamental rethinking that led us from style detection to substance verification.

posts.title: Building VulnRap: How We're Making Vulnerability Report Validation Actually Work for PSIRT Teams
posts.summary: A detailed walkthrough of four development sprints, what we built, what we learned, what works, and what's coming next.

posts.title: We Built the Tool We Wished Existed
posts.summary: Why we created VulnRap, how it helps with triage, and why you should feel comfortable using it.
